wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the RoadWatch AI project overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as an AI-powered civic intelligence and transparency platform for road safety governance. Document the core mission of combining computer vision, spending transparency, complaint automation, and civic chatbot functionality. Describe the target audience including citizens, municipal workers, and government agencies. Highlight the key value propositions: improved road safety, transparency, accountability, and automated civic processes. Include the technology stack overview, system architecture philosophy, and how the platform addresses real-world road infrastructure challenges. Provide context on the hackathon scope and future roadmap.

wiki_catalogs.name: Damage Detection Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Damage Detection Service that implements YOLOv8-based road damage detection. Document the computer vision pipeline including model loading, inference processing, and synthetic fallback algorithms. Explain the scene assessment system that validates image quality and determines road relevance. Detail the severity classification algorithm based on bounding box area calculations. Document the heuristic detection system for non-YOLO environments and the scene quality control mechanisms. Include performance metrics, inference time measurements, and accuracy considerations. Address image preprocessing, quality validation, and fallback strategies for demo mode operation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for RoadWatch AI core endpoints including /upload-image, /detect-damage, /calculate-score, and /get-road-data. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas using Pydantic models, authentication requirements, and error handling. Include detailed examples of image upload validation, damage detection payloads, road health score calculations, and road data retrieval. Explain parameter validation rules, status codes, and integration patterns. Provide client implementation guidelines and common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Flutter application architecture. Explain the MVVM pattern implementation with Provider-based state management, the application entry point structure, and theme configuration system. Document the architectural layers including presentation, business logic, and data access patterns. Cover the dependency injection setup, service initialization order, and global state management. Include architectural diagrams showing component relationships, data flow patterns, and the separation of concerns. Address design patterns used throughout the application and explain how they contribute to maintainability and scalability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Repository
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the DataRepository class that serves as the central abstraction layer for data access in RoadWatch AI. Document the dual-mode operation supporting both MongoDB integration and mock data fallback. Explain the initialization process, connection handling, and automatic data seeding. Detail all CRUD operations including get_roads, get_road, update_road_score, get_budgets, get_complaints, and complaint management functions. Document the search functionality including get_roads_nearby and get_road_network_nearby with geospatial calculations. Explain the complaint lifecycle management with timeline tracking and status updates. Include examples of data transformation, error handling, and fallback mechanisms. Address performance considerations, caching strategies, and connection pooling. Provide guidance on extending the repository with new data sources and implementing custom queries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for RoadWatch AI. Include detailed prerequisites covering Python 3.11+, Flutter SDK, Docker, and MongoDB requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for both backend and frontend components. Document environment setup, configuration files (.env), and dependency installation using requirements.txt and pubspec.yaml. Include development server startup procedures, port configurations, and initial project verification steps. Cover Docker Compose setup for local development, database initialization, and service orchestration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and platform-specific considerations for Windows, macOS, and Linux environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk Prediction Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Risk Prediction Service that uses Random Forest classifiers to forecast road deterioration. Document the machine learning pipeline including feature engineering, model training, and prediction algorithms. Explain the data preparation process using historical road condition data, weather patterns, traffic volume, and complaint frequency. Detail the model evaluation metrics, feature importance analysis, and prediction confidence scoring. Document the integration with external weather APIs and traffic data sources. Include model deployment considerations, performance optimization, and accuracy validation techniques. Address data preprocessing, feature scaling, and handling of missing values in the prediction workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complaint Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for complaint management endpoints including /generate-complaint, /complaints, /complaints/{complaint_id}/send, and /complaints/{complaint_id}/read. Document the complete complaint lifecycle from creation to authority submission. Include request schemas for ComplaintCreateRequest, response models for complaint tracking, and real-time update mechanisms. Explain status transitions, authority routing logic, and integration with the real-time update hub. Provide examples of complaint generation, status updates, and bulk operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Provider-based state management system. Explain the AppState class structure, provider patterns, and reactive state updates. Document async data loading strategies, error handling in state providers, and performance optimization techniques. Cover specific providers like ComplaintProvider and DetectionProvider, their responsibilities, and data flow patterns. Include examples of state subscription patterns, computed properties, and state synchronization across components. Address best practices for managing complex application state, memory management, and debugging state-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Pydantic data models that define the core data structures in RoadWatch AI. Document each model class including DetectionBox for AI detection results, Complaint for complaint lifecycle management, RoadSegment for road network data, RiskResponse for predictive analytics, and ChatResponse for AI assistant interactions. Explain field definitions, validation rules, type annotations, and serialization behavior. Detail the relationship between models and their usage in API responses and database operations. Include examples of model instantiation, validation, and conversion between different representations. Document optional fields, default values, and constraint validation. Explain how models integrate with FastAPI for request/response validation and how they support both JSON serialization and database mapping. Provide guidance on extending models and adding new validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for RoadWatch AI system design. Describe the high-level architecture including the Flutter frontend with mobile and web support, FastAPI backend service layer, and AI/ML processing components. Document the clean architecture pattern with presentation, application, domain, and infrastructure layers. Explain the component interactions, data flow between frontend and backend, and integration patterns with external services like YOLO, OpenAI, and MongoDB. Include system boundaries, microservice architecture considerations, and deployment topology. Address cross-cutting concerns like real-time communication via WebSockets, offline functionality, and demo-first behavior. Provide system context diagrams and component relationship explanations suitable for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complaint Management Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Automated Complaint Management Service that handles civic road issue reporting. Document the complaint routing logic that automatically assigns issues to appropriate municipal departments based on location and damage type. Explain the letter generation system that creates standardized complaint documents with jurisdiction-specific formatting. Detail the status tracking system that manages complaint lifecycle from submission to resolution. Document the timeline management functionality and authority communication protocols. Include the integration with geographic data for accurate department assignment and the fallback mechanisms for edge cases. Address complaint validation, duplicate detection, and escalation procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk Prediction Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for risk prediction endpoints including /predict-risk and related data endpoints like /get-budget-data and /get-road-network-data. Document the RiskRequest schema, prediction algorithms, and response formats. Include examples of weather impact modeling, traffic pattern analysis, and predictive deterioration risk assessment. Explain input parameters, model dependencies, and integration with historical data sources. Provide guidance on interpreting risk scores and making informed maintenance decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Screen Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the screen navigation system and routing patterns. Document the ShellScreen as the main navigation container, tab-based navigation structure, and route management. Explain screen lifecycle management, navigation state preservation, and deep linking capabilities. Cover specific screens including HomeScreen, CaptureScreen, and specialized modules. Include navigation patterns for different user workflows, modal presentations, and conditional navigation based on user roles. Address navigation performance optimization, route preloading, and handling navigation errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mock Data Ecosystem
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the mock data system that powers RoadWatch AI during development and demonstration phases. Document each mock dataset including roads data with health scores and locations, budget allocation records with transparency calculations, complaint records with full lifecycle tracking, risk features for machine learning training, demo detections for AI validation, and road network data for geographic context. Explain the data structure, field definitions, and relationships between datasets. Detail how mock data supports offline development, automated testing, and demo scenarios. Include examples of data usage patterns, query operations, and integration with the DataRepository. Document data generation strategies, consistency requirements, and maintenance procedures. Provide guidance on updating mock data for new features and maintaining data freshness for demonstration purposes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for RoadWatch AI backend endpoints. Document all RESTful API endpoints including POST /upload-image, POST /detect-damage, POST /calculate-score, POST /generate-complaint, GET /get-road-data, GET /get-budget-data, and POST /predict-risk. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas using Pydantic models, authentication requirements, and error handling. Include WebSocket endpoints like /ws/updates for real-time communication. Document request/response examples, parameter validation rules, status codes, and rate limiting considerations. Provide client implementation guidelines, authentication patterns, and integration examples. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chatbot Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Multi-LLM Chatbot Service that provides civic assistance through OpenAI, Groq, and Google AI models. Document the LLM integration architecture including model selection, prompt engineering, and response synthesis. Explain the fallback mechanism that switches between different AI providers when primary services fail. Detail the context-aware response system that maintains conversation history and adapts responses based on user queries. Document the citation system that references relevant road policies and procedures. Include the rule-based fallback system for common questions and the conversation management protocols. Address rate limiting, error handling, and response quality optimization across multiple AI providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for WebSocket real-time communication endpoints including /ws/updates. Document connection handling, message formats, event types, and real-time interaction patterns. Explain the RealtimeUpdateHub implementation, broadcast mechanisms, and client connection management. Include examples of event broadcasting for detect-damage, generate-complaint, and sync-offline operations. Document message payload structures, error handling, and connection lifecycle management. Provide client implementation guidelines for real-time updates and event subscription patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for reusable UI components and widget library. Document custom widgets including MapWidget for road visualization, ChartWidget for data representation, and LoadingIndicator for async operations. Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and customization options. Cover responsive design implementation, adaptive layouts for web and mobile, and accessibility features. Include widget testing strategies, performance optimization techniques, and component documentation standards. Address styling consistency, theme integration, and cross-platform UI considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend documentation for the Flutter application. Document the application structure including main.dart entry point, Provider-based state management, and screen navigation patterns. Explain the MVVM architecture with Provider pattern implementation, async data loading strategies, and error boundary handling. Document key screens including home, capture, chatbot, complaints, intelligence, and transparency modules. Cover component architecture, widget composition patterns, and reusable UI elements. Include state management best practices, performance optimization techniques, and offline-first functionality. Address responsive design considerations, accessibility compliance, and cross-platform deployment for web and mobile platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility and Health Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for utility and health endpoints including /health, /api-info, and search endpoints like /search-roads and /roads-nearby. Document system health monitoring, API metadata retrieval, and geographic search capabilities. Include examples of uptime monitoring, model information queries, and spatial search operations. Explain pagination parameters, filtering options, and performance considerations for large datasets. Provide integration examples for system monitoring and geographic data exploration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Offline Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for offline-first architecture and data synchronization. Explain LocalStorageService implementation, offline data persistence strategies, and queue management for failed requests. Document ConnectivityService for network monitoring and automatic retry mechanisms. Cover offline state detection, data conflict resolution, and synchronization strategies when connectivity is restored. Include examples of offline-capable workflows, data caching patterns, and user feedback during offline periods. Address performance considerations, storage optimization, and graceful degradation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI/ML Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for RoadWatch AI's artificial intelligence and machine learning services. Document the YOLOv8 integration for road damage detection, including model loading, inference pipeline, and synthetic fallback algorithms. Explain the risk prediction service using Random Forest classifiers for road deterioration forecasting. Detail the automated complaint management system with routing logic and letter generation. Document the multi-LLM chatbot integration supporting OpenAI, Groq, and Google models with fallback mechanisms. Include model training data preparation, performance optimization, accuracy metrics, and deployment considerations. Address computer vision preprocessing, scene assessment, quality control, and real-time inference capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Assistant Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the chat assistant endpoint /chat including ChatRequest and ChatResponse schemas. Document multi-LLM integration patterns, context-aware responses, and fallback mechanisms. Include examples of civic assistance queries, road-specific information retrieval, and conversation history management. Explain query processing, citation handling, and error recovery strategies. Provide implementation guidelines for chat integration and best practices for conversational AI interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for service layer integration and external API communication. Document ApiService for RESTful communication with backend services, LocationService for geolocation functionality, and WebSocketService for real-time updates. Explain service initialization patterns, error handling strategies, and retry mechanisms. Cover authentication integration, request/response transformation, and service mocking for testing. Include examples of service composition, dependency injection patterns, and service lifecycle management. Address performance optimization, caching strategies, and service monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data management documentation for RoadWatch AI. Document the DataRepository abstraction layer that handles both mock datasets and MongoDB integration. Explain the data models defined in Pydantic models including DetectionBox, Complaint, RoadSegment, RiskResponse, and ChatResponse schemas. Detail data transformation and validation processes, caching strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Document the mock data ecosystem including demo detections, budget records, complaints, and risk features. Include data lifecycle management, retention policies, and data migration strategies. Address data security, privacy considerations, and access control mechanisms. Provide examples of data querying patterns, aggregation operations, and integration with external data sources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for RoadWatch AI's real-time communication system. Document the WebSocket implementation for bidirectional real-time updates, including connection establishment, message formatting, and event broadcasting. Explain the event-driven architecture for live status notifications, multi-user synchronization, and instant UI updates. Detail the frontend WebSocket client implementation, connection management, and error recovery mechanisms. Document the backend WebSocket hub coordination, message routing, and scalability considerations. Include examples of real-time features like live complaint updates, road condition alerts, and collaborative monitoring. Address connection pooling, heartbeat mechanisms, reconnection strategies, and performance optimization for high-concurrency scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Offline Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for RoadWatch AI's offline-first architecture. Document the local data persistence strategy using Flutter's local storage services, including data synchronization queues and conflict resolution mechanisms. Explain the connectivity detection system and automatic synchronization when network becomes available. Detail the offline queue management for pending operations, retry logic, and data consistency guarantees. Document the demo-first behavior that ensures app functionality even when backend services are unavailable. Include offline data models, cache invalidation strategies, and performance optimization for limited connectivity scenarios. Address user experience considerations for offline states, data staleness indicators, and seamless online/offline transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation for RoadWatch AI. Document the backend testing approach using pytest, including unit tests, integration tests, and smoke tests for API endpoints. Explain the test configuration, mock data usage, and test environment setup. Detail the frontend testing strategy using Flutter widget tests and integration testing patterns. Document test coverage guidelines, continuous integration testing workflows, and automated testing in GitHub Actions. Include examples of testing AI/ML services, WebSocket functionality, and offline synchronization. Address testing best practices for concurrent operations, error scenarios, and performance testing. Provide guidance on writing effective tests for complex business logic and ensuring reliability across all system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation for RoadWatch AI. Document Docker containerization using the provided Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for multi-service orchestration. Explain production deployment strategies using Railway platform, including environment configuration, secrets management, and scaling considerations. Detail the GitHub Actions workflow for automated deployment to GitHub Pages for the frontend. Document environment variable configuration, database setup, and service dependencies. Include monitoring and logging setup, health check endpoints, and disaster recovery procedures. Address security considerations, SSL/TLS configuration, and CDN integration for static assets. Provide deployment checklists, rollback procedures, and performance optimization for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for RoadWatch AI contributors. Document code organization standards, naming conventions, and architectural patterns used throughout the codebase. Explain the FastAPI dependency injection system, Flutter Provider state management, and service layer architecture. Detail error handling patterns, logging strategies, and debugging techniques. Document performance best practices, memory management, and optimization techniques for both backend and frontend. Include code review guidelines, testing requirements, and contribution workflows. Address security coding practices, input validation, and data protection measures. Provide guidelines for extending the platform, adding new features, and maintaining code quality across the full-stack application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation for RoadWatch AI. Document common issues and their solutions including setup problems, dependency conflicts, and environment configuration errors. Address backend service startup failures, database connection issues, and API endpoint problems. Detail frontend compilation errors, Flutter dependency issues, and platform-specific deployment problems. Include debugging techniques for AI/ML services, WebSocket connection issues, and offline synchronization problems. Document performance troubleshooting for slow API responses, memory leaks, and UI rendering issues. Provide diagnostic tools, log analysis techniques, and error message interpretation guides. Include FAQ section addressing frequently encountered problems and their step-by-step resolution procedures.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# RoadWatch AI: AI-Powered Civic Road Safety Platform

## Project Introduction

RoadWatch AI is an innovative civic technology platform designed to enhance road safety transparency and efficiency through artificial intelligence. The project addresses critical challenges in urban infrastructure management by providing citizens and government agencies with intelligent tools for road condition monitoring, complaint automation, and predictive risk assessment.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as a bridge between citizens and municipal authorities, enabling real-time road condition assessment through AI-powered image analysis while automating bureaucratic processes for road maintenance and repair requests.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Road Condition Monitoring**: Automated detection of road damages using computer vision
- **Civic Transparency**: Real-time access to road maintenance data and budget allocation
- **Efficient Complaint Management**: AI-assisted automated filing and tracking of road-related issues
- **Predictive Analytics**: Risk assessment for road deterioration and maintenance prioritization
- **Offline-First Architecture**: Robust functionality for areas with limited connectivity

### Target Audience
- **Citizens**: End-users seeking to report road conditions and track maintenance progress
- **Municipal Authorities**: Government agencies managing road infrastructure and budget allocation
- **Urban Planners**: City officials requiring data-driven insights for infrastructure planning
- **Transportation Departments**: Maintenance crews needing priority-based work scheduling

## Technical Architecture

The RoadWatch AI platform follows a modern microservices architecture with clear separation of concerns across four distinct layers:

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        A[Flutter Mobile App]
        B[Web Interface]
    end
    
    subgraph "API Layer"
        C[FastAPI Backend]
        D[RESTful Endpoints]
    end
    
    subgraph "AI/ML Layer"
        E[YOLOv8 Detection]
        F[LLM Chatbot]
        G[Risk Prediction]
        H[Scoring Engine]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        I[Mock Datasets]
        J[MongoDB]
        K[Image Storage]
    end
    
    A --> C
    B --> C
    C --> E
    C --> F
    C --> G
    C --> H
    E --> I
    F --> I
    G --> I
    H --> I
    C --> J
    C --> K
```

### Component Breakdown

**Citizen Interface (Flutter)**: A responsive mobile application featuring map-based visualization, image capture capabilities, and intuitive workflow for road condition reporting.

**Governance API (FastAPI)**: A robust backend service providing RESTful endpoints for all platform functionalities including detection, scoring, complaint management, and data analytics.

**AI/ML Services**: Specialized machine learning components handling computer vision tasks, natural language processing, and predictive modeling.

**Data Layer**: Flexible storage system supporting both mock datasets for development and production-ready databases for real-world deployment.

### Design Patterns

The architecture implements several key design patterns:
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted data access layer for database operations
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic encapsulation and dependency injection
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time updates via WebSocket connections
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic service instantiation based on configuration

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/main.py` - Initializes FastAPI application with CORS middleware, GZip compression, and static file serving for uploaded images.

**Frontend Entry Point**: `roadwatch_ai/frontend/lib/main.dart` - Configures Flutter application with Material Design theming, state management, and service initialization.

### Core Modules

**Configuration Management**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/core/config.py` - Centralized settings management using Pydantic BaseSettings with environment variable support.

**API Router**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/routers/api.py` - Comprehensive REST API with 20+ endpoints covering detection, scoring, complaints, risk prediction, and real-time updates.

**Service Container**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/services/container.py` - Dependency injection system managing service lifecycle and inter-service communication.

**Data Repository**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/db/repository.py` - Unified data access layer supporting both mock datasets and MongoDB integration.

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**: `roadwatch_ai/docker-compose.yml` - Docker-based deployment with configurable environment variables for different environments.

**Frontend Dependencies**: `roadwatch_ai/frontend/pubspec.yaml` - Flutter package management with 27+ dependencies including state management, mapping, and real-time communication.

### External Dependencies

**Backend Stack**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/requirements.txt` - Modern Python ecosystem including FastAPI, Pydantic, Ultralytics YOLO, OpenAI, and MongoDB drivers.

**Frontend Stack**: Comprehensive Flutter ecosystem supporting offline functionality, real-time communication, and responsive design.

### Integration Points

**Real-time Communication**: WebSocket endpoints (`/ws/updates`) enabling live updates for detection results, complaint status changes, and system notifications.

**Multi-LLM Support**: Pluggable architecture supporting OpenAI GPT, Groq Llama, and Google Gemini models for chatbot functionality.

**Image Processing Pipeline**: Integrated YOLOv8 detection with fallback heuristic algorithms for robust performance across diverse image conditions.

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "User Interface"
        UI[Flutter App]
    end
    
    subgraph "API Gateway"
        API[FastAPI Router]
    end
    
    subgraph "Business Services"
        DET[Damage Detection]
        CMP[Complaint Service]
        RSK[Risk Prediction]
        CHAT[Civic Chatbot]
        SCR[Scoring Engine]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        REP[Data Repository]
        DB[(MongoDB)]
        FS[(File System)]
    end
    
    UI --> API
    API --> DET
    API --> CMP
    API --> RSK
    API --> CHAT
    API --> SCR
    DET --> REP
    CMP --> REP
    RSK --> REP
    CHAT --> REP
    SCR --> REP
    REP --> DB
    DET --> FS
    CMP --> FS
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**AI-Powered Road Damage Detection**: Advanced computer vision system capable of identifying potholes, cracks, and other road defects with confidence scoring and severity assessment.

**Automated Complaint Generation**: Intelligent complaint creation system that captures location data, timestamps, and image evidence with standardized templates for municipal processing.

**Real-time Risk Prediction**: Predictive analytics engine assessing road deterioration risks based on weather patterns, traffic volume, and historical complaint data.

**Civic Transparency Dashboard**: Comprehensive data visualization showing budget allocation, actual road conditions, and maintenance priorities.

**Offline-First Architecture**: Seamless operation without internet connectivity with automatic synchronization when connection is restored.

### Implementation Highlights

**Detection Pipeline**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/services/detection.py` - Sophisticated YOLOv8 integration with synthetic fallback algorithms ensuring reliable performance.

**Complaint Workflow**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/services/complaint.py` - Structured complaint processing with timeline tracking and authority ticket simulation.

**Real-time Updates**: `roadwatch_ai/backend/app/routers/api.py` - WebSocket-based live updates for detection results and complaint status changes.

**State Management**: `roadwatch_ai/frontend/lib/providers/app_state.dart` - Comprehensive Flutter Provider implementation managing application state across multiple screens.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> OfflineMode
    OfflineMode --> OnlineMode: Network Available
    OfflineMode --> Detection: Capture Image
    OfflineMode --> Complaint: File Complaint
    
    OnlineMode --> Detection: Upload Image
    OnlineMode --> Complaint: File Complaint
    OnlineMode --> RiskAssessment: Request Analysis
    
    Detection --> Scoring: Process Results
    Scoring --> UpdateDashboard: Display Score
    Complaint --> TrackStatus: Monitor Progress
    RiskAssessment --> PriorityQueue: Show Priority
    
    UpdateDashboard --> [*]: Sync Complete
    TrackStatus --> [*]: Complaint Resolved
    PriorityQueue --> [*]: Action Taken
```

The RoadWatch AI platform represents a comprehensive solution for modern urban infrastructure management, combining cutting-edge AI technologies with civic transparency principles to create a more efficient and accountable system for road maintenance and public safety.

**Sources:**
- [main.py](roadwatch_ai/backend/app/main.py)
- [config.py](roadwatch_ai/backend/app/core/config.py)
- [api.py](roadwatch_ai/backend/app/routers/api.py)
- [container.py](roadwatch_ai/backend/app/services/container.py)
- [main.dart](roadwatch_ai/frontend/lib/main.dart)
- [requirements.txt](roadwatch_ai/backend/requirements.txt)
- [pubspec.yaml](roadwatch_ai/frontend/pubspec.yaml)
- [docker-compose.yml](roadwatch_ai/docker-compose.yml)
- [detection.py](roadwatch_ai/backend/app/services/detection.py)
- [repository.py](roadwatch_ai/backend/app/db/repository.py)
- [app_state.dart](roadwatch_ai/frontend/lib/providers/app_state.dart)
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](roadwatch_ai/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)

</blog>
